title: Understanding the Components of Acceleration
text: To find the total acceleration of the car, you need to consider both the tangential and centripetal components of acceleration.

title: Tangential Acceleration
text: The tangential acceleration is the rate at which the speed of the car is decreasing. Convert the rate from km/h² to m/s².

title: Centripetal Acceleration
text: The centripetal acceleration depends on the speed of the car and the radius of the curve. Use the formula for centripetal acceleration.

title: Calculate Centripetal Acceleration
text: Use the formula a_c=v²/r, where v is the speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$r$$ is the radius in meters. Convert $$60.0$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ to $$\frac{m}{s}$$ first.

title: Total Acceleration
text: The total acceleration is the vector sum of the tangential and centripetal accelerations. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the magnitude.

title: Calculate Total Acceleration
text: Use the formula a=sqrt(a_t²+a_c²), where $$a_t$$ is the tangential acceleration and $$a_c$$ is the centripetal acceleration.
